LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF DCFS TRUST'S MOTION FOR AMENDMENT OF JUDGMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. JUDGMENT DEBTOR OSCAR H. MARTINEZ WAS NOT SERVED. THE DOCUMENTS ATTACHED TO CHEN DECLARATION AS EXHIBIT 5 SUGGEST ELVIRA MARTINEZ MAY NOT BE HAVE BEEN SERVED WITH THIS MOTION AT THE CORRECT ADDRESS. FURTHER, THERE IS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE IN THE CHEN DECLARATION TO ESTABLISH THAT ELVIRA Y MARTINEZ ALSO GOES BY THE NAME ELVIRA VELASQUEZ. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WITH THE NAME AND ADDRESS OF THE PREVAILING PARTY'S COUNSEL OR THE PREVAILING PARTY IF PRO PER IN THE TOP LEFT OF THE FIRST PAGE OF PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jun-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Philip Kilduff, Esq. 415-673-0368 specially appearing for Plaintiff DCFS.
No other Appearnce.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested and Discussed. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF DEFENDANT ELVIRA Y. MARTINEZ aka ELVIRA Y. VELASQUEZ.

Upheld. Granted in part. Defendant to pay 100 per pay period.

Order is signed.* (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

*After the hearing, the Court orders the Levying officer to return $100 to the Judgment Creditor and the rest of the amount to the Judgment Debtor.*

Motion For Amendment Of Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alisa Kim, Esq. (415-826-9160) for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DCFS TRUST's Motion For Amendment Of Judgment is GRANTED. No opposition filed.
Order and amended Judgment signed. (see scanned order and amended Judgment for details)
Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, email:rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)

Motion For Amendment Of Judgment; Declaration Of David T. Chen In Support
Off Calendar Dec-17-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF DCFS TRUST'S Motion For Amendment Of Judgment is denied without prejudice. Judgment debtor Oscar H. Martinez was not served. The documents attached to Chen Declaration as Exhibit 5 suggest Elvira Martinez may not be have been served with this motion at the correct address. Further, there is insufficient evidence in the Chen Declaration to establish that Elvira Y Martinez also goes by the name Elvira Velasquez. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Judge: Marla Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Motion For Amendment Of Judgment; Declaration Of David T. Chen In Support
Off Calendar Nov-27-2012 Continued To Dec-17-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS A.E. HESKETH, TEL. NO. 756-0335 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DCFS TRUST'S Motion For Amendment Of Judgment. The hearing is continued to December 17, 2012. Pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B, the moving party shall provide a courtesy copy of its papers by December 7, 2012. Those papers shall be lodged with department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Susan Crozier, CSR # 4761 = (302/MJM)
